rationale: Both candidates independently converge on the two real hotspots human reviewers debated (audio_vae.py config mutation, patch_emission.py +1→+2 gating), but X also raises the stale README documentation tied to the dropped language/方言 mapping — the same concern amy-why-3459 raised about docs/tests for that change — while Y omits it entirely and frames everything as a clean APPROVE. X's findings are more concrete and actionable (specific boundary-value test cases, exact deepcopy fix, exact README line), though it overstates severity on the config-mutation item that maintainers ultimately deemed intentional/harmless, and has one redundant duplicate finding. Y is slightly more precise/calibrated (correctly treats resolved items as non-blocking) but is thinner on recall and gives vaguer 'consider'/'worth a comment' suggestions rather than concrete fixes.
